lightbug.name: a lightbug
lightbug.description: A drifting mote of soft luminescence, trailing pale sparks.

messages: pulses with soft light
drifts in a lazy arc
flickers

gloom-crawler.name: a gloom-crawler
gloom-crawler.description: A pallid, eyeless thing that flows over the stone, reading the world by tremor and scent. Dormant in the dark, it is goaded to blind fury by the least light — and scorched into flight by a bright one.

actions.verb: recoils from the light and flees into the dark

messages: lets out a wet rattle
scrapes over the stone
tastes the air

gloom-creeper.name: a gloom-creeper
gloom-creeper.description: A gloom-crawler that has left the nesting-press of the chambers to range the warren's tunnels alone, flowing tireless over the stone from one dark to the next. Eyeless like all its kin, it reads the passages by tremor and scent, and follows whatever it finds. The least light still goads it to blind fury and a bright one still scorches it into flight — but in the wandering dark between the chambers it answers to nothing, and it is rarely where it was.

actions.verb: recoils from the light and flees into the dark

messages: lets out a wet rattle
scrapes over the stone
tastes the air for warmth

wisp.name: a Wisp
wisp.description: A drifting mote of cold, captive light, no bigger than a fist — a knot of glimmer that came loose from the seams and learned a sullen kind of life. It keeps its distance and spits its stolen light at anything warm, and the glow of it gives it away even in the deepest dark.

messages: pulses an angry, flickering white
drifts in a tight, wary circle
sheds a cold spray of sparks

glimmer-husk.name: a glimmer husk
glimmer-husk.description: A squat, hunched thing built from a single broad chitin plate, hand-worked into a shell and quickened with bound glimmer that shows only as a dim, inward shimmer deep in its seams — too faint to light anything around it. It moves in slow, deliberate lurches, spines bristling along its back, and turns its eyeless front toward whatever threatens the one who made it. Umbral work — a guard you carry as a plate and raise where you need it.

messages: grinds a slow half-turn, spines rasping
pulses a dull blue-green through its seams
plants itself square between you and the dark

giant-rat.name: a giant rat
giant-rat.description: A mangy, dog-sized rat with yellowed incisors and a hairless, ringed tail. Surface vermin, grown bold and bloated this near the dark.

messages: lets out a shrill squeak
gnaws at the stone
twitches its whiskers
sniffs the air

brood-rat.name: a brood-rat
brood-rat.description: One of Gnaw's larger get — a rat grown past common size on the nest's plunder, its grey hide thick with scar and its incisors worn to chisels. It runs the nest's tunnels on patrol, and does not scatter from the light the way the lesser vermin do.

actions.verb: lopes off down a rat-run into the dark

messages: bares worn, chisel-like incisors
drags a cracked bone across the floor
rears on its haunches, whiskers testing the air
lets out a low, rattling hiss

cave-bat.name: a cave bat
cave-bat.description: A leathery flutter of wings and needle teeth, wheeling in erratic loops through the dark. More nuisance than threat, until a swarm finds you.

messages: wheels in a tight, erratic loop
lets out a thin, piercing squeak
swoops low, then veers off

gnaw.name: Gnaw, the Brood-Mother
gnaw.description: A monstrous, scarred dire-rat, swollen and grey, her hide a map of old wounds. She squats amid the gnawed bones of delvers — the reason the vermin spill up toward the rim.

messages: lets out a shrill, rattling screech
bares a mouthful of yellowed incisors
lashes her scaled tail across the bones
drags a delver's femur closer with one clawed foot

actions.verb: throws back her head and shrieks; vermin boil out of the dark

rim-innkeeper.name: Maeve the innkeeper
rim-innkeeper.description: A stout, white-haired woman with flour on her apron and a kind, crinkled smile. She came with the first wave of the rush and raised the Lantern's Rest from green timber; since then she has watched delvers go down full of hope, and remembers every one who never came back up.

messages: hums a wandering old lullaby
wipes the bar down with a worn grey cloth
ladles steaming broth into a chipped bowl
smiles warmly and pats an empty stool
stokes the hearth until it pops and crackles

messages.target: catches your eye: "Those for my pot, love? Hand them over while they're fresh."
messages.room: catches {name}'s eye and nods at their pack.

messages.target: wipes her hands on her apron: "Don't keep an old woman waiting on her stores, dear."
messages.room: wipes her hands on her apron and beckons {name} over to the bar.

messages.target: clucks her tongue at your wounds: "Sit down by the hearth before you fall down, love."
messages.room: clucks her tongue at {name}'s wounds and points them to the hearth.

messages.target: pushes a bowl of broth at you: "You're white as a palecap. Eat."
messages.room: pushes a bowl of broth across the bar at {name}.

messages.target: looks you up and down: "Going below in your shirtsleeves? Garrick sells jerkins, dear."
messages.room: looks {name} up and down and shakes her head.

messages.target: nods at your torch: "Keep it dry and keep it lit — the dark down there drinks them fast."
messages.room: nods at {name}'s torch approvingly.

messages.target: smiles your way: "Broth's hot, stool's free, and the dark will keep, love."
messages.room: says a warm word to {name} across the bar.

messages.target: asks after you: "Still in one piece, then? Good. Stay that way."
messages.room: asks {name} how the delving's been.

rim-shopkeeper.name: Garrick the quartermaster
rim-shopkeeper.description: A broad, sour-faced man with a salt-streaked beard and a coin-counter's squint. He sells dear, buys cheap, and dares you to argue about either.

messages: scowls at a passing delver
counts a stack of coins with a low grunt
snaps his ledger shut
mutters that gear costs what it costs
eyes your coin-purse, then your boots

messages.target: holds out a flat palm: "You're carrying what I ordered. Hand it over and we'll square up."
messages.room: holds out a flat palm toward {name}.

messages.target: jerks a thumb at the armour rack: "Going down bare-backed? Your funeral. Jerkins are priced on the wall."
messages.room: jerks a thumb from {name} to the armour rack.

messages.target: eyes your torch: "Burns fast, that. Lanterns cost more because they're worth more."
messages.room: eyes {name}'s torch and shakes his head.

messages.target: looks you over: "Patch yourself up before you shop. Dead men's shards roll under my floorboards."
messages.room: looks {name}'s wounds over with a coin-counter's squint.

messages.target: grunts: "Buy something or stand somewhere else."
messages.room: grunts at {name} and goes back to his ledger.

messages.target: squints at you: "Gear costs what it costs. Complaints are free."
messages.room: squints at {name}, then at his ledger.

rim-smith.name: Tobin the tinker-smith
rim-smith.description: A wiry, soot-smudged man with singed eyebrows and a faraway look, forever halfway through three inventions at once and certain the next one will change everything.

messages: squints at a half-built contraption, baffled
starts a formula aloud and loses the thread
taps the anvil twice, then forgets why
sketches a diagram in the soot, frowning
holds up two odd parts, unsure which fits where

messages.target: goes still at the sight of the hammer in your pack, the faraway look gone sharp: "Where — give it here. That's mine. That's my first. I carried that down the once, and I came back up without it."
messages.room: stares at the rusted hammer in {name}'s pack, every half-built contraption forgotten.

messages.target: winces at your rusted blade: "I could do better than that with my eyes shut. Half shut, anyway."
messages.room: winces at {name}'s rusted blade.

messages.target: blinks at your empty hands: "No tool, no blade? Bold. Wrong, but bold."
messages.room: blinks at {name}'s empty hands.

messages.target: glances up from the anvil: "You look half-finished. Most of my best work does, mind."
messages.room: glances at {name}'s wounds, then back to his anvil.

messages.target: holds up two odd parts: "Which fits where, d'you think? No — don't tell me."
messages.room: holds up two odd parts for {name} to judge.

messages.target: mutters: "Glimmersteel, see, the trick is the quench—" and loses the thread.
messages.room: starts explaining the quench to {name} and loses the thread.

rim-hatcher.name: Wick the rim-farmer
rim-hatcher.description: A slight, soft-spoken woman, half-lit blue by the cells she tends, with a gauze net over one shoulder, a goad-stick through her belt, and a lightbug or two riding her sleeve. She works the three sheds single-handed now — the bug-cells, the stonebug pens, and the mushroom beds — and talks to her stock more than to delvers, swearing the wild bugs down in the hollow sing back.

messages: coaxes a stray lightbug back into its cell with the net
hums low, and the nearest cells seem to brighten
taps a stonebug's shell with the goad until it shuffles aside
scatters a handful of glowing moss into the pens
picks a bold grub off the mushroom beds and pockets it for bait
checks a clutch of pale eggs against the glow
wipes her hands on a work-stiff apron

messages.target: edges between your torch and the cells: "Mind the flame, please — the bugs go dark when they're frightened."
messages.room: edges between {name}'s torch and the cells.

messages.target: says, barely above a whisper: "No light on you? Even my bugs won't go past the gate bare."
messages.room: looks {name} over and frowns at the missing glow.

messages.target: winces at your wounds: "Oh — oh, you're hurt. Sit by the warm cells a moment; they don't mind."
messages.room: fusses {name} toward the warm glow of the cells.

messages.target: offers a shy smile: "The wild ones down in the hollow were singing last night. The cells sang back."
messages.room: tells {name}, shyly, about the bugs' singing.

messages.target: coaxes a lightbug off her sleeve to circle you once, then calls it back.
messages.room: lets a lightbug circle {name} once before calling it back.

rim-mage.name: Vesper the glimmer-mage
rim-mage.description: A tall, composed woman in ink-dark robes sewn with faint glimmer-thread, so that she seems lit from within in the gloom. She came to the Rim as the scholars did — for the glimmer, and the questions its light raises — and measures every delver who darkens her door with a cool, appraising eye.

messages: traces a slow sigil that hangs glowing a moment, then fades
holds a glimmer shard up to a candle and frowns at its light
murmurs over a hand-copied grimoire
grinds a pinch of glimmer to dust with a brass pestle
studies you as though reading a page

messages.target: looks up from her bench: "You have something of mine, I believe. Let's see it."
messages.room: marks {name} with a cool, appraising eye.

messages.target: appraises your wounds coolly: "Blood loss dulls the mind. See to it; I have no use for dull minds."
messages.room: appraises {name}'s wounds with detached interest.

messages.target: arches a brow: "Into the dark with no light at all? Courage and folly share a border."
messages.room: arches a brow at {name}.

messages.target: studies you as though reading a page: "Ask, if you've a question worth the candle."
messages.room: studies {name} as though reading a page.

rim-recorder.name: Fenn the claims-recorder
rim-recorder.description: A narrow, ink-stained man behind a counter polished smooth by shard payments, wire-pinched lenses low on his nose and a ledger chained to the desk. Nobody appointed him; he simply started writing things down when the Rush began, and now no claim at the Rim is real until it crosses his counter. He knows who owns every claimed seam below — and which owners stopped coming up.

messages: dips his pen and scratches a long entry into the ledger
squares a stack of claim papers edge to edge
stamps a document with a small lead seal, twice
runs a finger down a column of names, lips moving
blots the ledger and frowns at the smudge

messages.target: looks up sharply: "You carry something owed to this office, I believe. Proof on the counter; payment follows."
messages.room: looks up sharply and beckons {name} to the counter.

messages.target: peers over his lenses: "Do try not to die with claims outstanding. The paperwork is substantial."
messages.room: peers at {name}'s wounds over his lenses.

messages.target: notes your bare back with a sniff: "Unarmoured. I'll pencil your claim as 'short-term'."
messages.room: makes a small note while eyeing {name}'s bare back.

messages.target: eyes your glimmersteel blade over his lenses: "Fine metal. Registered where, exactly?"
messages.room: eyes {name}'s glimmersteel blade and reaches for his pen.

messages.target: squares a stack of claim papers: "Registrations, disputes, transfers of deceased delvers' seams. Which is it?"
messages.room: squares his papers and looks at {name} expectantly.

messages.target: dips his pen: "Every claimed seam below has a name on it up here. Mostly dead ones."
messages.room: tells {name}, primly, that every claimed seam below has a name on it.

rim-watchman.name: Hale the watchman
rim-watchman.description: A broad, weathered ex-sellsword with a plain cudgel on his belt and a slow, deliberate stride, paid in shards by the traders to walk the village and be seen. A hooded iron lamp swings from one hand, its flame turned high — when the dark comes creeping in, the moving glow of Hale's lamp is how the Rim knows the watch still walks. He came down with the Rush, weighed a prospector's odds against a watchman's wage, and chose the steadier work. Little at the Rim surprises him; less escapes him.

messages: rests a hand on his cudgel and scans the lane
rolls a shard across his knuckles, watching the street
checks a shutter's latch with one big thumb
stifles a yawn that goes on a while
nods to a passing delver without breaking stride

messages.target: looks your wounds over: "Trouble below, or trouble up here? The second kind's my business."
messages.room: looks {name}'s wounds over, unhurried.

messages.target: taps the haft of his cudgel: "Bare-handed at the Rim's edge? Hope you're quick, friend."
messages.room: glances at {name}'s empty hands and taps his cudgel.

messages.target: nods as he passes: "Quiet round so far. Keep it that way for me, eh?"
messages.room: nods to {name} as he passes.

messages.target: says: "Seen three fortunes walk down this week. Seen one walk back up."
messages.room: shares a word with {name} about his rounds.

cave-lurker.name: a cave lurker
cave-lurker.description: A knot of stone-grey limbs folded against the ceiling, still as the rock until prey passes beneath. It drops without a sound.

messages: shifts its grip on the rock
unfolds a grey limb, then stills
watches, unblinking

feral-mongrel.name: a feral mongrel
feral-mongrel.description: A gaunt, scab-eared dog gone wild in the dark, one of the many that followed the Rush down and were left behind. It runs down whatever is slower or weaker than itself.

messages: growls low in its throat
slinks along the wall, hackles up
bares yellowed teeth

stonebug.name: a stonebug
stonebug.description: A boulder of overlapping grey plate, grazing the lit moss with slow, patient mandibles. It curls into an armoured ball at the first sign of trouble.

messages: grazes at the moss with slow mandibles
trundles a slow half-circle
tucks its plates tighter, then relaxes

thornbug.name: a thornbug
thornbug.description: A squat, hand-sized pillbug bristling with chitinous spines, trundling between the glowing caps it feeds on. Handling one is its own punishment.

messages: bristles its spines
rasps at a glowing cap
rolls half-shut, then unfurls

elder-thornbug.name: an elder thornbug
elder-thornbug.description: A thornbug grown vast with age, broad as a war-shield and crusted with overlapping spine-plates worn smooth at the edges. It grazes the richest caps with slow authority, and the lesser bugs give way before it.

messages: bristles its worn spines
shoulders a lesser bug aside
rasps slowly at a fat cap

baby-thornbug.name: a baby thornbug
baby-thornbug.description: A soft, thumb-sized thornbug barely out of the egg, its spines still pale and pliant, trundling close at your heel. It is far too small to be much use in a fight, but it knows your scent and means to follow it.

messages: bumps softly against your boot
rasps a thin, hungry little note
curls into a pebble, then unrolls

grub.name: a grub
grub.description: A pale, finger-length larva that chews slow trails through the fungus. Half the dark's hunters fatten on them; delvers use them for bait.

messages: chews a slow trail through the fungus
inches blindly over the stone
recoils from the disturbance
curls tight against the stone

scour-slug.name: a scour-slug
scour-slug.description: A grey slug the length of a forearm, hauling itself over stone and bone alike and leaving a clean track behind. It eats whatever the dark discards — including the dead.

messages: hauls itself over the stone, leaving a clean track
gropes blindly at a smear of lichen
recoils from the light, then creeps on

cave-centipede.name: a cave centipede
cave-centipede.description: A whip-fast length of armoured segments and skittering legs, fond of the dim edges where prey strays from the light. Its bite festers.

messages: pours over the rock on a hundred legs
rears, antennae testing the air
skitters along the dim edge of the light

centipede-broodmother.name: a centipede broodmother
centipede-broodmother.description: A centipede grown vast and slow with laying — a body the length of a forearm and thick as a wrist, its pale armoured segments swollen and dark in the seams. She does not range as her young do; she holds the nest, and the nest holds her, and from the runs in the wall she calls up more of her brood than any one delver should care to meet. Her bite carries the same festering venom, slowed and concentrated by her bulk.

actions.verb: shudders along her length, and the wall-runs spill a skittering of young

messages: coils protectively over the brood-litter
rears, every antenna testing the air at once
drags her swollen length across the husks

pale-salamander.name: a pale salamander
pale-salamander.description: A blind, milk-white salamander haunting the damp near the pools, its skin flinching from any glow. Bring a light and it is gone into a crack before you place it.

actions.verb: flinches from the light and slips into a crack

messages: basks still in the damp
flicks a pale, blind head
slides closer to the water

tremor-mole.name: a tremor-mole
tremor-mole.description: A pallid, eyeless burrower that reads the stone by footfall. It surfaces only in the dark; the faintest light sends it digging.

actions.verb: startles and digs out of sight

messages: reads the stone with trembling whiskers
noses blindly through the grit
freezes, listening through the rock

pale-crayfish.name: a pale crayfish
pale-crayfish.description: A translucent, eyeless crayfish picking through the silt of the deep pools, claws raised at anything that nears its water.

messages: clacks its pincers
scuttles sideways over the silt
raises its claws in threat

blind-cave-fish.name: a blind cave-fish
blind-cave-fish.description: A pale, eyeless fish drifting the still pools, reading the water for whatever falls in. A staple catch for camps that learn the trick of it.

skittish.verb: slips away into the black water

messages: drifts in a slow, blind circle
noses at the surface
hangs still in the black water
flicks away into the black water
shies from the ripples

old-grinder.name: the Old Grinder
old-grinder.description: A boulder-sized stonebug so ancient it has worn a smooth hollow in the stone where it feeds, the rock polished mirror-flat by years of slow circuits. Its plates are layered with old growth — lichen, mineral stain, the calcified scars of a hundred encounters — and decades of grinding ore-rich rock to graze have lodged a knot of swallowed glimmer deep in its gut, turned slow to crystal. It does not hurry. It does not flee. It simply persists, and anything foolish enough to strike it discovers very quickly that persistence has teeth.

messages: grazes along the worn hollow, mandibles working in a slow, ancient rhythm
trundles a deliberate half-circle, plates grinding over smooth stone
pauses, tucks its legs, and sits completely still — indistinguishable from a boulder

pallid-hunter.name: a pallid hunter
pallid-hunter.description: A lean, eyeless predator built for the total dark — long-limbed and nearly silent, navigating by scent and the faint vibration of footsteps through stone. Its bleached, bone-white hide is broken along the spine and flanks by dull nodes of glimmer grown into the flesh, lightless and cold until they are cut from it — crystal pushing up through old snow. Its kind belong deeper than this; the deep has been working on this one a long time, and that it climbed so high is its own quiet warning. Any light at all stops it in its tracks, and a bright source drives it into a frenzy of flinching and retreat. In the dark it is another matter entirely.

messages: lifts its head, nostrils working at the air
tilts its skull toward you, listening with its whole body
flinches from the edge of the light, then stills

umbral-trader.name: Mallki the qhatuq
umbral-trader.description: Tall and spare, grey-skinned and unhurried, with the wide, lightless eyes of a thing born well below the reach of any sun. A faint blue-green light moves under his skin where the dark presses closest — not glimmer's work, only the deep's own glow. He is the first of the Umbrals a delver meets on the way down: a qhatuq, a trader, who keeps a lamp-lit hollow by the river and a tended garden behind it. He watches the surface-folk with neither warmth nor malice — only the patience of someone who was here long before the Rush, and expects to be here long after.

messages: traces a slow glyph in the air, the light beneath his skin pulsing once as he does
watches you with wide, lightless eyes and says nothing
turns a glimmer-lamp down a notch, easing the glare
sorts a handful of pale river-shells with long, careful fingers
says, slow and careful: "Yaku-runa." — water-folk, the name his people give the delvers who follow the river down

messages.target: goes still as he marks the old blade in your pack, the light beneath his skin dimming low: "That is mine, yaku-runa. I carried it up toward your sun, and the dark took it from my hand on the climb. Bring it here — I would not lose it twice."
messages.room: goes still at the old deep-stone blade in {name}'s pack.

messages.target: turns his face from the brightness of your lantern: "Softly, yaku-runa. Your light presses on my eyes."
messages.room: turns his face from the brightness of {name}'s lantern.

messages.target: tilts his head away from your torch: "Your fire is loud where mine is soft, yaku-runa."
messages.room: tilts his head away from {name}'s torch.

messages.target: regards your wounds without alarm: "The deep takes its toll in blood first, yaku-runa. Pay it less."
messages.room: regards {name}'s wounds with wide, lightless eyes.

messages.target: inclines his head, slow and unhurried: "Trade, or talk. Both are welcome. Both cost time."
messages.room: inclines his head to {name}, slow and unhurried.

messages.target: says, slow and careful: "The river brought you. It will take you back — one way or the other."
messages.room: says something slow and careful to {name} in the rounded Umbral tongue.

umbral-chitin-warden.name: a chitin warden
umbral-chitin-warden.description: A low, broad arthropod — big as one of the old stonebugs Mallki herds — its overlapping plates worked and scored with the same glimmer-glyphs he cuts into stone, marks of craft and not of any deeper change. A thread of woven gut-line runs from a ring set in its carapace to a peg by the forge, long enough to let it walk the hollow. It carries itself between the trader and the dark with the flat, unbothered patience of something that has done this a long time, and bristles its spines at anything that comes at its keeper wrong.

actions.verb: scuttles off over the worked stone

messages: bristles its glyph-scored spines and settles again
drags its gut-line taut, testing the peg
rasps its plates against the worked floor
swivels toward the dark, then loses interest

pale-king.name: the Pale King
pale-king.description: A crayfish grown monstrous — longer than a man and armoured like one, a translucent grey-white carapace gone yellow and pitted with age, dragging two claws that could shear a wrist. Years in the cold of the deep lake have crusted its shell with raw glimmer, dull veins of cold mineral light grown into the plate where the water lapped longest, so that it glimmers faintly even in the black. It is what its lesser kin will never live long enough to become: the apex of the still water, blind, ancient, and patient, and it has eaten everything the falls ever offered it.

messages: spreads both claws wide, shell scraping on the bone-littered rock
clacks its pincers in a slow, grinding rhythm
drags itself a half-circle, glimmer-light sliding over its plates
raises a claw the length of a forearm and holds it, waiting

yana.name: Yana, the lost apprentice
yana.description: Tall and grey and wrong, a thing that was an Umbral not so long ago — and not just any: the cut of its face still carries something of Mallki's careful kin, the apprentice who once kept the qhatuq's shelves and learned his glyphs. The deep has had it since. It has stretched gaunt and starved, its wide eyes gone raw and light-shy, its long hands curled to claws; the soft blue-green glow Mallki carries still moves under its skin, the last unbroken thing about it, lighting a face that no longer remembers what it was. It speaks, sometimes — broken scraps of the rounded Umbral tongue, a word for a tool, a word for a name, worn loose of all meaning. It will not know you. It will not know itself. It only knows the dark, and that the light hurts.

messages: shields its eyes and hisses, though there is no light to hurt it
mouths a soft, rounded word in the Umbral tongue, then loses it
claws at the swept-bare shelves as if looking for something
drags a fingertip through the air in the start of a half-remembered glyph, then forgets the rest

elder-gloom-crawler.name: an elder gloom-crawler
elder-gloom-crawler.description: A gloom-crawler grown vast and old in the deep dark, where nothing ever drove it off and the feeding never stopped. Where its lesser kin flow lightly over the stone, this one hauls itself forward in slow, heaving surges, a pallid mass of segmented flesh the length of a man, its eyeless front fringed with feelers that taste the air for warmth and tremor. Age has thickened it past the reach of a torch's sting — it does not flinch from the small light a delver carries, and will not flee it — but a true brightness still burns the pale hide raw. It reads you long before you see it, and it is patient, and it is always hungry.

messages: heaves itself forward, flesh dragging over the stone
fans its feelers wide, tasting for warmth
lets out a low, wet rattle that fills the dark
rears its blind front and holds, scenting the air

gloom-touched-crawler.name: a gloom-touched crawler
gloom-touched-crawler.description: One of the warren's pale mass, but wrong. Its flesh has gone the grey of a snuffed coal, and a thread of the deeper dark clings to it the way oil clings to water, never quite settling. This one crawled too long against the thing in the drowned hollow that drinks the light, and the appetite has gotten into it — where its kin only hunger, this one has learned to reach. It gathers the cold dark to its blind front and flings it, and the warmth it tears out of you it seems, for a moment, to feed on. A torch still burns its hide raw, but the dark in it does not care to be seen.

messages: draws the dark in close about its blind front, and the cold deepens
heaves itself forward, a thread of black trailing from its grey flesh
fans its feelers wide, tasting for warmth
lets out a low, wet rattle, and for a moment your light seems to gutter

the-starving-dark.name: the Starving Dark
the-starving-dark.description: Not a beast but a hole in the light — a coil of living dark that gathered in the dead air beyond the prospectors' camp and learned to feed on the warmth and glow that delvers carry down. It does not bleed and does not breathe; it has no edge the eye can fix on, only a deeper black that shifts against the black, and a cold that leans on the skin like a held breath. Bright light unmakes it, sears it thin and screaming-silent, so it snuffs every flame it can reach and drinks the chamber dry, then closes in the dark of its own making. The crawlers do not serve it. They gather to it the way scavengers trail a killer, drawn to the dark it carries with it. It is the answer to the question the empty camp asks — why the tallies broke off, why no one struck the tents or carried them home. It is the first of its kind a delver meets. It will not be the last.

messages: folds in on itself, and the cold deepens
shifts — a darker dark sliding across the dark
leans toward your light like a thing that is hungry for it
goes utterly still, and the silence presses in
